top_signals.title: Sinopec completes Hong Kong’s largest bio-methanol bunkering
top_signals.summary: Hong Kong-based bunker supplier Sinopec has delivered 1,000 mt of bio-methanol to a methanol dual-fuel roll-on/roll-off (RoRo) vessel owned by China Merchants Guangzhou RoRo Shipping. The fuel was supplied to the CM Shenzhen by Wah Kwong-operated bunker vessel Da Qing 268, marking the “largest single green methanol bunkering” in Hong Kong so far. The bio-methanol ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Trump reimposes US blockade and demands 20% Hormuz shipping fee
top_signals.summary: Trump declared the US would collect a 20% fee on all Hormuz cargo as “Guardian of the Strait,” flipping the script on Iran’s own toll demands, while US forces struck dozens of targets in Iran and the IRGC hit Bahrain, Jordan, Kuwait and Oman in the biggest exchange since the April ceasefire. US President Donald ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Fed’s Waller says another high inflation reading would be “signal”
top_signals.summary: Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller said today that he will place more weight on another high inflation reading than a lower one when reviewing data published this week. Waller explained that inflation readings have shown five to six consecutive months of increases. “If I get another higher one, I’m gonna treat that as signal, not ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Cavotec signs EUR 7 million order to retrofit container vessels for shore power
top_signals.summary: Cavotec has signed an order valued at EUR 7 million with a global container shipping company to retrofit vessels for shore power. The order covers the retrofit of low- and medium-voltage container vessels with shore power technology. The retrofitting work will be carried out while the vessels remain in operation, so as not to interrupt ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Hormuz transits remain subdued; Asia-US container rates rise, tanker rates steady to softer
top_signals.summary: Vessel traffic remains subdued through the Strait of Hormuz as negotiations continue between the US and Iran, but US President Donald Trump said in a social media post that the ceasefire was over. Hostilities reignited on Thursday after the two nations had previously signed a memorandum of understanding (MoU) and entered a ceasefire. According to ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: UAE condemns Iran’s ‘brazen’ attack on tankers as US launches fresh strikes
top_signals.summary: The UAE accused Iran of a “brazen” attack on two tankers in the Strait of Hormuz on Monday, killing one and wounding eight, as Donald Trump said the US would impose a 20% charge as part of a new blockade on the waterway. The US president said they were hitting Iran “very hard” as strikes ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Port Of Los Angeles Offers $75 Million With Zero-Emission Truck Purchasing Incentive Project
top_signals.summary: The Port of Los Angeles is seeking proposals for its Zero-Emission Truck Purchasing Incentive Project—eligible to all Licensed Motor Carriers (LMCs) currently registered in the Port Drayage Truck Registry—for the purchase of at least 10 battery-electric, Class 8 drayage trucks. This purchasing incentive will provide up to $300,000 per battery-electric truck, with a maximum award ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Princess Anne visits HD Hyundai as UK seeks to revive shipbuilding
top_signals.summary: Princess Anne, the younger sister of Britain’s King Charles III, visited HD Hyundai Heavy Industries’ (HHI) shipyard in Ulsan on Tuesday, touring its shipbuilding and engine manufacturing facilities and discussing maritime cooperation between Korea and the United Kingdom. The princess arrived in Korea on Monday for a three-day visit to mark the 75th anniversary of ...
